nav.personasSub: Your character — who you are in the story

common.comingSoon: This screen ships in a later milestone.
common.offline: You're offline. Messages will send once the connection is back.

update.downloading: Downloading update… the app will restart itself

chat.title: Chats
chat.empty: No chats yet. Create your first one.

newChat.noConnectionsHint: First create at least one connection in Settings → Connections.
newChat.noCharactersHint: First create or import at least one character on the Characters page.
newChat.gameLanguageHelp: Language the AI writes responses in.

list.deleteConfirm: Delete this chat and all its history?

room.empty: No messages yet. Write the first one.
room.inputPlaceholder: Write a message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line)

errors.noConnection: This chat has no connection selected. Set one from the chat list.
errors.offline: You're offline. Check your internet connection and try again.
errors.rateLimitRetry: The model's request limit is exhausted. Try again in about {{seconds}} s (button below).
errors.rateLimit: The model's request limit is exhausted (free plans often have a daily cap). Wait a bit, or switch to another model in the chat settings.
errors.badKey: The provider rejected your API key. Check it in Settings → Connections.
errors.overloaded: The model service is currently overloaded or down. Try again in a moment.
errors.modelNotFound: Model “{{model}}” doesn't exist or your key can't access it. Pick another one in the chat settings.
errors.modelNotFoundGeneric: The selected model doesn't exist or your key can't access it. Pick another one in the chat settings.

suggest.tooltip: Let the model suggest 3 ways to react (costs one extra request).

room.branchHint: Creates a copy of the chat ending at this message — the story can go a different way from here, the original chat stays untouched.
room.branchConfirm: Create a story branch from this message? The original chat stays unchanged.

group.title: Group members
group.removeLastError: Can't remove the last member — a group needs at least one.
group.autoHint: In auto mode, whichever member is mentioned by name in the last message replies; otherwise whoever has been silent the longest.

promote.hint: Turns an NPC from the ledger and chat history into a full character and adds it to the group.
promote.error: Card generation failed. Try again or adjust the input.

director.title: Scene direction
director.hint: Silent guidance for the narrator — applies from the next message.

editor.deleteConfirm: Delete this character? Chats pointing to it will keep working but lose the reference.
editor.systemPromptHint: Leave empty to use the default RP instruction.

help.name: The character's name as the AI uses it when writing their lines and as shown in the chat.
help.description: The character's core description — appearance, background, key traits. Sent to the AI in every prompt, so keep it concise. ⚙ Best in English (AI instruction).
help.personality: A short summary of personality traits (e.g. "quiet, watchful, dry sense of humor"). Helps the AI play the character consistently. ⚙ Best in English (AI instruction).
help.scenario: A description of the starting situation or setting the chat begins in. Sets context before the first message is sent. 🖊 In your game's language (story content).
help.firstMes: The message the character sends first when you start a new chat. It's the first thing you see, so it should showcase the character's tone and style. 🖊 In your game's language (story content).
help.alternateGreetings: Extra opening-message variants you can pick from instead of the default one when starting a chat. 🖊 In your game's language.
help.mesExample: Example dialogue that teaches the model this character's writing style and reply length. Never sent as a visible chat message. 🖊 In your game's language (story content).
help.systemPrompt: A custom system instruction just for this character — overrides the app's default general RP instruction. ⚙ Best in English (AI instruction).
help.postHistoryInstructions: Text inserted into the prompt after the entire chat history, right before the model's reply. Use it for rules the model should follow right now. ⚙ Best in English (AI instruction).
help.creatorNotes: The card creator's internal notes. Never sent to the model — for your (or whoever you share the card with) reference only.
help.tags: Labels used for filtering and searching in the character gallery. Have no effect on the AI's behavior.

personas.title: Personas
personas.subtitle: A persona is how the AI sees you — its name replaces the "user" placeholder in cards and lorebook entries, and its description is added to the system message.
personas.empty: No personas yet. Create your first one.

fields.name: Name
fields.appearancePlaceholder: What the persona looks like — hair, eyes, build, clothing…

form.isDefaultBadge: This is the default persona for new chats.
form.deleteConfirm: Delete this persona? Chats pointing to it will keep working but lose the reference.

lorebooks.title: Lorebooks
lorebooks.empty: No lorebooks yet. Create one or import a World Info file.

editor.deleteConfirm: Delete this lorebook, including all its entries and links?

help.name: The lorebook's name, for your own orientation in the list. Has no effect on the AI's behavior.
help.description: An optional note on what the lorebook is for (e.g. "world history and geography"). For your reference only.
help.entryKeys: Keywords that trigger this entry when they appear in the recent chat — the entry's content then gets inserted into the prompt (World Info). E.g. "castle, king, throne".
help.entrySecondaryKeys: Extra keywords — if set, the entry only triggers when a primary key AND a secondary key both appear in the chat (more precise, stricter triggering).
help.entryContent: The text inserted into the prompt when this entry triggers — a fact, a place or character description, a world rule, etc.
help.entryComment: An optional note about the entry for your own reference. Never sent to the model.
help.entryPriority: Controls the order triggered entries are ranked in when they don't all fit the context budget. Higher priority = more likely to make it into the prompt.
help.entryAlwaysOn: When enabled, the entry is always inserted into the prompt, regardless of whether its keywords appeared in the chat.
help.entryCaseSensitive: When enabled, keywords must match the chat text's letter case exactly (e.g. "King" won't match "king").
help.entryEnabled: A disabled entry never triggers, even if its keywords appear in the chat. Useful for turning an entry off temporarily without deleting it.

editor.linksHint: A lorebook can be linked to a specific character, a specific chat, or globally (applies to every chat).

facts.canonUnlockWarn: Tut-tut… you locked this rule as unbreakable. Unlocking lets the extractor change it and the story may drift away from it. Really unlock?
facts.canonDemoteWarn: Tut-tut… the game verified this rule through repeated play. Revoking canon stops guarding it against drift. Really revoke?
facts.canonDeleteWarn: Tut-tut… you are deleting a canon rule of the story. Without it the world may quietly drift. Really delete?

prompt.empty: No request sent yet — after the first message, this shows exactly what the AI received.
prompt.overBudgetHint: The prompt exceeds the context budget, so history, summary and facts get trimmed more than they should. You can raise this connection's "Context budget" in Settings — but in moderation: the whole prompt is sent with every message, so a higher budget means slower replies and, on the free tier, hitting the tokens-per-minute limit sooner. 8,000-16,000 works well for the free tier; go higher only with a paid key.
prompt.noneTrimmed: Nothing was trimmed — everything fit the budget.
prompt.driftTitle: Silent drift corrections in this prompt

search.noResults: Nothing found — maybe nothing is indexed yet.
search.unavailable: Semantic search unavailable: the (extraction or main) connection must be Gemini or OpenAI-compatible; Claude offers no embeddings.
search.reindexHint: After changing the embedding model, recomputes all vectors for this chat.
search.backfillHint: One-off indexing of an old chat's history so it becomes semantically searchable.

chronicle.empty: No chronicle entries yet. Send some messages or add facts to populate the timeline.

usage.title: Usage statistics
usage.subtitle: Estimated token usage and API request counts. Tokens are just an estimate — free tier mostly limits the number of requests per day.
usage.hint: Tokens are a rough estimate (based on text length), not an exact figure from the API. On the free tier, today's request count matters most — it's what counts against the daily quota.

backup.subtitle: A backup saves the database and character avatars into one ZIP file. Importing a backup restores it — overwriting your current data.
backup.exportHint: Creates a ZIP with everything: chats, characters, personas, lorebooks, memory, and avatars.
backup.importHint: Pick a ZIP backup and stage it for import. Changes only take effect after restarting the app.
backup.importWarning: Importing will overwrite all current data (chats, characters, personas, lorebooks, memory, and avatars) with the backup's contents. This cannot be undone. Continue and restart the app?
backup.pendingRestart: An import is staged — it will take effect after the app restarts.
backup.autoBackupHint: On app start a ZIP backup is created in the backups folder and old backups are rotated out.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: App errors are automatically written to a file — attach it when reporting a bug.
diagnostics.updateDownloading: Downloading and installing… the app will restart itself
diagnostics.updateError: Update check failed — try again later.
diagnostics.logLevelHint: Controls what gets written to the log file (see above) — the browser console always shows everything, this only limits the file.
diagnostics.logLevelDebug: Debug — very detailed, for actively hunting a bug.
diagnostics.logLevelError: Error — only major failures, recommended for normal play.

memory.subtitle: Default memory engine settings. The extraction connection can be overridden per chat in the Memory panel.

embedding.title: Semantic memory (embeddings)
embedding.subtitle: Vector retrieval of relevant facts and older scenes. Requires a Gemini or OpenAI-compatible connection.

help.extractionInterval: How often (after how many messages) the engine scans the recent chat and pulls durable facts (names, relationships, promises…) into the memory ledger. A lower number means memory updates more often, but costs more API calls.
help.verbatimWindow: How many of the most recent messages stay in the prompt verbatim, unchanged. Older messages get summarized instead, to save context space. A larger window means the AI sees the recent dialogue more precisely, but consumes more tokens.
help.embeddingModel: The model that turns text into vectors for semantic memory search. Leave blank to auto-select based on the connection's provider.
help.topK: How many of the most relevant memories are inserted into the prompt at most, per message. More means richer context, but more tokens spent.
help.minScore: The similarity threshold (0–1) below which a memory is considered irrelevant and not used. A higher value is a stricter filter — only very similar memories get used.

connections.title: Connections
connections.subtitle: Presets for API providers. The key is stored only in the system keyring, never in the database.
connections.empty: No connections yet. Add one to test a connection.

fields.name: Name
fields.baseUrlHint: Leave empty to use the default OpenAI endpoint.

help.name: A custom label for this connection, just for your own orientation in the list (e.g. "Gemini Free"). Has no effect on the AI's behavior.
help.provider: Who provides the API — Gemini, Claude, or an OpenAI-compatible service (ChatGPT, DeepSeek, OpenRouter, and others that use the same interface).
help.model: The specific model of that provider to call (e.g. gemini-2.0-flash). Click "Load models" to pick from the list of supported models.
help.baseUrl: The API endpoint address for OpenAI-compatible services. Leave empty for official OpenAI; fill in only if you're using a different compatible service (DeepSeek, OpenRouter, a local server…).
help.temperature: Higher means more creative, less predictable answers. Keep it low (0.3–0.7) for factual answers; 0.8–1.0 works well for RP.
help.topP: Limits word choice to the most likely portion. Usually leave at default; tune either temperature or top P, not both.
help.maxTokens: The maximum length of the model's response, in tokens. A higher value allows longer answers, but increases wait time and cost.
help.contextBudget: The maximum amount of context tokens (chat history, character card, memory…) sent to the model per request. The whole prompt is re-sent with every message — a higher budget therefore means slower replies and, on the free tier, hitting the tokens-per-minute limit sooner (it does not affect the daily request quota). It also doesn't improve the game's memory: key facts are protected by the trim order and the canon reminder at the end of the prompt. The recommended range is 8,000-16,000 for the free tier; going higher only makes sense with a paid key.
help.apiKey: The provider's API access key. Stored encrypted in the system keyring, never in the app database, and not shown again after saving.
help.purpose: What this connection is used for — chat, image generation, or embeddings.

apiKey.neverShown: Once saved, the key is never shown again — only its presence is verified.

connections.deleteConfirm: Delete this connection? Its saved key will be deleted too.

illustrations.title: Illustrations
illustrations.subtitle: Automatic illustration generation for inventory items and locked facts.
illustrations.enabledHelp: When enabled, the app automatically generates illustrations for new items and locked facts using the Gemini API.
illustrations.limitHelp: 0 = unlimited. Maximum number of auto-generated images per day.
illustrations.connectionHelp: Gemini connection used for image generation. If none selected, the default chat connection is used.

presets.title: Prompt presets
presets.subtitle: Named system-prompt additions + parameter overrides you can assign per chat.
presets.empty: No presets yet. Create your first one.

fields.name: Name
fields.extraSystemPromptPlaceholder: Additional instructions appended to the system prompt…
fields.authorNotePlaceholder: Injected near end of prompt — use for style guidance, formatting rules, or tone reminders…
fields.regexRulesHelp: JSON array of find/replace rules. Format: [{"id":"1","pattern":"text to find","replacement":"replace with","enabled":true}]

presets.regexTestPlaceholder: Paste sample text to test regex rules...

sync.title: Sync
sync.subtitle: Folder sync lets two MySillyTavern instances share data through a shared folder (Syncthing, Dropbox, Nextcloud…). Every change is written to a journal file and applied on the other device on startup.
sync.folderHelp: Pick a folder that is synced across your devices. Leave empty to disable sync.
sync.deviceIdHelp: Automatically generated — identifies this device in the sync folder.
sync.disabled: Sync disabled — set a folder path to enable.

tts.title: Sound
tts.subtitle: Text-to-speech settings for reading replies aloud.
tts.autoReadHelp: When enabled, new AI replies are read aloud automatically.
tts.backendEdgeHelp: Online — higher quality, needs internet. Falls back to Web Speech on error.
tts.backendWebSpeechHelp: Offline — always works, uses system voices.
